crossEventQuestion: When should Microsoft authorize a major share repurchase while preserving capital flexibility?
observation: The reviewed 2013 and 2024 events authorize bounded repurchase capacity as part of a continuing cash-return commitment.
invariant: Authorize shareholder cash return within an explicit ceiling while retaining discretion over actual deployment.
boundaryConditions: The authorization ceiling and competing investment or liquidity needs must remain explicit.
unresolvedConflicts: The bounded public event set contains no reviewed Amy Hood event suspending repurchases on this same capital-return axis.

decisionAxis.decisionQuestion: When should bounded repurchase capacity be authorized?

conditions: A continuing cash-return commitment is paired with an explicit authorization ceiling.

conditionDelta: No reviewed opposite-condition repurchase event is available in the bounded public evidence set.
actionDelta: Defer or reduce cash return when capital flexibility or competing investment needs dominate.
